explorer.label: Explorer
explorer.expertModeDesc: Enable advanced explorer controls only if you want to work with internal files.
explorer.expertModeActive: Advanced controls are now available on this screen and at the bottom of the explorer.
explorer.showSystemFilesDesc: Hides internal files and prevents visual noise for most users.
explorer.showSystemFilesVisible: You will see YAML, JSONL, claude.md and other advanced artifacts alongside memories.
explorer.showSystemFilesHiddenDesc: Keeps the explorer focused on memories, although expert mode remains available.

update.title: Updates
update.desc: Check for new versions of AI Context OS
update.checkDesc: See if a newer version is available
update.downloadInstall: Click to download and install the update
update.readyDesc: The update has been installed. Restart to apply it.

backup.title: Backup & Restore
backup.desc: Export or import your entire workspace as a .zip file
backup.exportDesc: Save a backup of the entire workspace
backup.restoreDesc: Import a .zip file to replace the current workspace
backup.restoreConfirm: Restore backup? Current files will be overwritten.

inference.title: Inference Provider
inference.desc: Configure optional BYO AI for inbox proposals today and local chat/runtime reuse later.
inference.heuristicFallback: If no provider is active, Inbox will keep using heuristic proposals.

search.placeholder: Search memories by id, description or tags...
search.hint: ↑↓ navigate · Enter to open · Esc to close

location.title: Workspace location
location.desc: You can create a new workspace or point the app at a folder you already use, including a personal archive or an Obsidian/Logseq vault.
location.useExistingDesc: Choose your current root folder and prepare AI Context OS inside it.
location.createNewDesc: Start with an initial structure tailored for AI Context OS.
location.existingHint: Best if you already keep information in organized folders. The app will work from that root without forcing a prior migration.
location.newHint: The app will create a new root and add a few starter folders to help you begin.
location.systemFiles: AI Context OS will add or update its system files if they are missing: .ai/, claude.md, .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, inbox/, and sources/.
location.compatibility: An Obsidian or Logseq vault can be used as the root. Your notes stay where they are; Markdown files with compatible frontmatter can be indexed as memories.
location.structure: System infrastructure: inbox/ · sources/ · .ai/ (rules, journal, tasks, scratch)

profile.title: Your profile
profile.desc: It will be saved as context memory for your AIs.

template.title: Template
template.desc: Pre-designed skills and rules. Customizable later.
template.developerDesc: Code review, debugging, architecture. Conventions & stack.
template.creator: Creator
template.creatorDesc: Writing, LinkedIn, newsletters, repurposing. Brand & voice.
template.customDesc: Empty workspace. Configure it your way.

confirm.title: Summary
confirm.desc: The app will prepare this location to work as an AI Context OS workspace and add its base infrastructure.

explorer.dragToMove: Drag to move to another folder
explorer.deleteFolder: Are you sure you want to delete the folder "{{name}}"?
explorer.deleteFile: Are you sure you want to delete the file "{{name}}"?
explorer.errorReserved: This folder is reserved for system or protected content
explorer.errorCopyPath: Could not copy path to clipboard

inbox.subtitle: Capture first, classify later, promote only when ready.
inbox.empty: No inbox items yet. Capture text, links or files to start the pipeline.
inbox.emptySelection: Select an inbox item to inspect or edit it.
inbox.dropDesc: Desktop drops work when the OS exposes file paths. You can always use Import files.
inbox.dropUnavailable: This drop source does not expose filesystem paths. Use Import files instead.

compose.textBody: Paste notes, snippets or rough content here...

proposal.title: Proposal
proposal.empty: No pending proposal yet. You can normalize the item first or generate one now.

ai.title: Proposal engine
ai.hint: Works with heuristics or BYO AI

tasks.notesHint: Use bullets (- ), lists, or free text to expand context.
tasks.emptyAll: No tasks. Create a new one above.

empty.title: Select a file to edit
empty.description: Use the sidebar explorer to open a note or file.

history.pendingSync: There are pending changes to synchronize.

graph.clickToInspect: Click a node to inspect. Double-click to open.

governance.godNodesDesc: Memories whose graph connectivity (degree) significantly exceeds their assigned importance. Consider increasing their importance score.
governance.noGodNodes: No god nodes detected — importance scores are well-calibrated

confirm.archiveAllDesc: These memories haven't been accessed in a long time and will be permanently deleted.
confirm.archiveDesc: This memory will be permanently deleted.
confirm.clearAllDesc: These temporary files have exceeded their TTL and will be permanently deleted.
confirm.deleteDesc: This scratch file will be permanently deleted.

health.needsUsageData: Some scores need usage data — connect an AI tool and send a few requests to calibrate.

dimensions.coverageDesc: % of memories ever surfaced by the router
dimensions.efficiencyDesc: How well loaded memories match actual queries
dimensions.cleanlinessDesc: Absence of expired scratch / decay candidates

observability.noRequests: No context requests yet. Connect an AI tool to get started.

intelligence.efficiencyStrong: Context efficiency is strong at {{value}}% — memories are well-calibrated for the queries coming in.
intelligence.efficiencyNeutral: Efficiency at {{value}}% — some memories may be loaded without matching query context.
intelligence.efficiencyDown: Efficiency low at {{value}}% — consider running an optimization analysis to recalibrate.

optimizations.noOptimizations: No pending optimizations — your context OS looks well-tuned.

simulation.title: Simulation
simulation.noResults: Type a query and simulate to see which memories would load.

vault.title: Vaults
vault.switchConfirmDesc: The application will reload into the selected vault. Unsaved changes will be lost.
vault.removeConfirm: Remove "{{name}}" from the vault list? The folder will not be deleted.

connectors.title: Connectors
connectors.description: Integrate AI Context OS with your AI tools.

claudeDesktop.name: Claude Desktop
claudeDesktop.description: Native app (recommended). Cowork session with automatic access to context and MCP.
claudeDesktop.cap1: Cowork: the optimal way to work with your files
claudeDesktop.cap2: Automatic stdio MCP (get_context, save_memory, get_skill, log_session)

claudeCode.name: Claude Code
claudeCode.description: CLI integration: Native MCP with 4 tools + automatic context.
claudeCode.cap2: Automatic reading of claude.md when opening the workspace

cursor.name: Cursor
cursor.description: Remote HTTP MCP when the app is open, plus static .cursorrules fallback when MCP is unavailable.
cursor.cap1: HTTP MCP on port 3847 / SSE (same 4 tools, requires app running)
cursor.cap2: Auto-generated .cursorrules for static fallback and no-MCP use

windsurf.name: Windsurf
windsurf.description: Remote HTTP MCP when the app is open, plus static .windsurfrules fallback when MCP is unavailable.
windsurf.cap1: HTTP MCP on port 3847 / SSE (requires app running)
windsurf.cap2: Auto-generated .windsurfrules for static fallback and no-MCP use

codexCli.name: Codex CLI
codexCli.description: Native stdio MCP for OpenAI/Codex terminal workflows.
codexCli.cap1: stdio MCP via Codex CLI (get_context, save_memory, get_skill, log_session)
codexCli.cap2: Works directly in the workspace root with local file access

chatgptWeb.name: ChatGPT Web
chatgptWeb.description: No MCP or local file access. Use manual context snapshots and handoff files.
chatgptWeb.cap2: Generate handoff.md for a new chat

chatgpt.name: ChatGPT / Codex
chatgpt.description: Full integration via Codex CLI with native MCP support. Also manual handoff for ChatGPT web.
chatgpt.cap1: stdio MCP via Codex CLI (get_context, save_memory, get_skill, log_session)
chatgpt.cap2: Codex works directly in the root folder with file access
chatgpt.cap3: Manual handoff for ChatGPT web (copy/paste context)

geminiCli.name: Gemini CLI
geminiCli.description: Native MCP via Gemini CLI. Same 4 tools as Claude Code.
geminiCli.cap1: stdio MCP via Gemini CLI (get_context, save_memory, get_skill, log_session)
geminiCli.cap2: Automatic reading of context from workspace root

geminiWeb.name: Gemini Web
geminiWeb.description: No MCP or local file access. Use manual transfer of optimized context.

copilot.name: GitHub Copilot
copilot.description: No native MCP. Static fallback only applies when Copilot is used inside editors that already read generated rules files.
copilot.cap1: Static context via generated rules files in compatible editors
copilot.cap2: Copy optimized context to clipboard for standalone chats

sections.coworkDesc: It's the optimal and easiest way to use our system. Just open the project with the Claude desktop app and use the <bold>Cowork</bold> tab. Claude will natively access all context and local files.
sections.mcpStdioDesc: To access your knowledge base from any conversation, add AI Context OS to claude_desktop_config.json:
sections.coworkMcpInfo: <bold>Cowork + MCP:</bold> We recommend using Cowork as the base. If you add MCP configuration, you will have advanced tools like global searches or graphs at your disposal.
sections.mcpRegisterDesc: Register AI Context OS as a permanent MCP server. This gives Claude Code access to all 4 tools (get_context, save_memory, get_skill, log_session).
sections.openWorkspaceDesc: Opens Claude Code in your workspace. The claude.md is auto-loaded as context, but MCP tools are not available without Option 1.
sections.terminalTip: <bold>Tip:</bold> Use both options together — register the MCP server once, then open the workspace with Option 2 for full context + tools.
sections.mcpHttp: Option 1 — HTTP MCP (dynamic, requires open app)
sections.mcpHttpDescCursor: Add to .cursor/mcp.json in your project:
sections.cursorrulesStaticDesc: The .cursorrules file is auto-generated at the root of the workspace with rules and base context. Cursor reads it automatically without configuration.
sections.windsurfrulesStaticDesc: The .windsurfrules file is auto-generated at the root of the workspace. Windsurf reads it without additional configuration.
sections.mcpVsRules: <bold>MCP vs .cursorrules:</bold> MCP gives dynamic access to scoring, memories, and tools. .cursorrules is static but does not require the app to be running.
sections.codexCliDesc: Codex CLI supports native MCP servers. Configure in ~/.codex/config.yaml:
sections.codexInfo: <bold>Codex CLI</bold> runs OpenAI models with direct MCP access. It's the best way to use AI Context OS with the ChatGPT/OpenAI ecosystem.
sections.chatgptWebDesc: For ChatGPT web without Codex, generate a context snapshot and paste it at the <bold>beginning of a new conversation</bold> before your first message.
sections.geminiMcpDesc: Install <linkGemini>Gemini CLI</linkGemini> and register AI Context OS as a permanent MCP server:
sections.geminiInfo: Gemini CLI supports MCP natively. Once registered, all 4 tools are available in every Gemini CLI session.

bridge.manualTransfer: <bold>Manual Transfer</bold> — Prepares the context to copy it or save it as a file when there is no native integration.
bridge.copilotNote: Note: .cursorrules is read by Cursor even with Copilot enabled. It is not read by GitHub Copilot in VS Code — use the clipboard handoff below instead.
bridge.handoffSuccess: File saved to .ai/scratch/handoff.md and copied to clipboard.

handoff.title: # Handoff for {{name}}
handoff.instructions: This document contains the active context of my memory workspace. Use it as a reference to continue the work in {{name}}.

nullInfo.title: Workspace not detected.
nullInfo.description: Configuration snippets require an initialized workspace. Go to <boldSettings>Settings → Workspace</boldSettings> to set up your workspace first.
